entries.name: repr
entries.code:
proc repr(x: bool): string {.magic: "BoolToStr", noSideEffect, raises: [],
                             tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: repr for a boolean argument. Returns x converted to the string "false" or "true".

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr(x: char): string {.noSideEffect, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
repr for a character argument. Returns x converted to an escaped string.

assert repr('c') == "'c'"

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr(x: string | cstring): string {.noSideEffect, raises: [].}
entries.description: repr for a string argument. Returns x converted to a quoted and escaped string.

entries.name: repr
entries.code:
proc repr[Enum: enum](x: Enum): string {.magic: "EnumToStr", noSideEffect,
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
repr for an enumeration argument. This works for any enumeration type thanks to compiler magic.

If a repr operator for a concrete enumeration is provided, this is used instead. (In other words: Overwriting is possible.)

entries.name: reprDiscriminant
entries.code:
proc reprDiscriminant(e: int): string {.compilerproc, raises: [], tags: [],
                                        forbids: [].}

entries.code: proc repr(p: pointer): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: repr of pointer as its hexadecimal value

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr(p: proc | iterator {.closure.}): string
entries.description: repr of a proc as its address

entries.name: repr
entries.code: template repr[T: distinct | (range and not enum)](x: T): string

genericParams.name: T
genericParams.types: distinct or range and not enum

entries.name: repr
entries.code: template repr(t: typedesc): string

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T: tuple | object](x: T): string {.noSideEffect, raises: [].}
entries.description:
Generic repr operator for tuples that is lifted from the components of x. Example:

$(23, 45) == "(23, 45)"
$(a: 23, b: 45) == "(a: 23, b: 45)"
$() == "()"

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T](x: ref T | ptr T): string {.noSideEffect, raises: [].}

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T](x: set[T]): string
entries.description:
Generic repr operator for sets that is lifted from the components of x. Example:

${23, 45} == "{23, 45}"

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T](x: seq[T]): string
entries.description:
Generic repr operator for seqs that is lifted from the components of x. Example:

$(@[23, 45]) == "@[23, 45]"

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T, IDX](x: array[IDX, T]): string
entries.description: Generic repr operator for arrays that is lifted from the components.

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T](x: openArray[T]): string
entries.description:
Generic repr operator for openarrays that is lifted from the components of x. Example:

$(@[23, 45].toOpenArray(0, 1)) == "[23, 45]"

entries.name: repr
entries.code: proc repr[T](x: UncheckedArray[T]): string
